An outsourced cloud service register contains the following for each outsourced cloud service:

- cloud service provider’s name
- cloud service’s name
- purpose for using the cloud service
- sensitivity or classification of data involved
- due date for the next security assessment of the cloud service
- contractual arrangements for the cloud service
- point of contact for users of the cloud service
- 24/7 contact details for the cloud service provider.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Only community or private clouds are used for outsourced SECRET and TOP SECRET cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Outsourced cloud service providers and their TOP SECRET cloud services, including sensitive compartmented information cloud services, undergo a security assessment by ASD assessors (or their delegates), using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the security assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Service providers, including any subcontractors, provide an appropriate level of protection for any data entrusted to them or their services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security requirements associated with the confidentiality, integrity and availability of data are documented in contractual arrangements with service providers and regularly reviewed to ensure they remain fit for purpose.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The right to verify compliance with security requirements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The right to verify compliance with security requirements documented in contractual arrangements with service providers is regularly exercised.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Break clauses associated with failure to meet security requirements are documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The requirement for service providers to report cyber security incidents to a designated point of contact as soon as possible after they occur or are discovered is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A minimum notification period of one month by service providers for significant changes to their own service provider arrangements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Types of data and its ownership is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The regions or availability zones where data will be processed, stored and communicated, as well as a minimum notification period for any configuration changes, is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access to all logs relating to an organisation’s data and services is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The storage of data in a portable manner that enables backups, service migration and service decommissioning without any loss of data is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A minimum notification period of one month for the cessation of any services by a service provider is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An organisation’s systems are not accessed or administered by a service provider unless a contractual arrangement exists between the organisation and the service provider to do so.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If an organisation’s systems are accessed or administered by a service provider in an unauthorised manner, the organisation is immediately notified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A cyber security strategy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Organisational-level cyber security documentation is approved by the chief information security officer while system-specific cyber security documentation is approved by the system’s authorising officer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A system’s security architecture is approved prior to the development of the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cyber security documentation is reviewed at least annually and includes a ‘current as at \[date\]’ or equivalent statement.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cyber security documentation, including notification of subsequent changes, is communicated to all stakeholders.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Systems have a system security plan that includes an overview of the system (covering the system’s purpose, the system boundary and how the system is managed) as well as an annex that covers applicable controls from this document and any additional controls that have been identified and implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Systems have a cyber security incident response plan that covers the following:

- guidelines on what constitutes a cyber security incident
- the types of cyber security incidents likely to be encountered and the expected response to each type
- how to report cyber security incidents, internally to an organisation and externally to relevant authorities
- other parties that need to be informed in the event of a cyber security incident
- the authority, or authorities, responsible for investigating and responding to cyber security incidents
- the criteria by which an investigation of a cyber security incident would be requested from a law enforcement agency, the Australian Signals Directorate or other relevant authority
- the steps necessary to ensure the integrity of evidence relating to a cyber security incident
- system contingency measures or a reference to such details if they are in a separate document.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Systems have a change and configuration management plan that includes:

- the establishment and maintenance of authorised baseline configurations for systems
- what constitutes routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- how changes to the configuration of systems will be requested, tracked and documented
- who needs to be consulted prior to routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- who needs to approve routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- who needs to be notified of routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- what additional change management and configuration management processes and procedures need to be followed before, during and after routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Systems have a continuous monitoring plan that includes:

- conducting security assessment activities to identify vulnerabilities
- analysing identified vulnerabilities to determine their potential impact
- implementing mitigations based on risk, effectiveness and cost.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
At the conclusion of a security assessment for a system, a security assessment report is produced by the assessor and covers:

- the scope of the security assessment
- the system’s strengths and weaknesses
- security risks associated with the operation of the system
- the effectiveness of the implementation of controls
- any recommended remediation actions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: At the conclusion of a security assessment for a system, a plan of action and milestones is produced by the system owner.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Classified systems are secured in facilities that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in server rooms or communications rooms that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in security containers suitable for their classification taking into account the combination of security zones they reside in.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Server rooms, communications rooms and security containers are not left in unsecured states.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Keys or equivalent access mechanisms to server rooms, communications rooms and security containers are appropriately controlled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Physical security is implemented to protect network devices in public areas from physical damage or unauthorised access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An authorised RF and IR device register for SECRET and TOP SECRET areas is developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unauthorised RF and IR devices are not brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security measures are used to detect and respond to unauthorised RF devices in SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An authorised photographic and video recording device register for SECRET and TOP SECRET areas is developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unauthorised photographic and video recording devices are not brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An authorised medical device register for SECRET and TOP SECRET areas is developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Medical devices authorised to be brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas meet, at a minimum, the following criteria:

- are listed on the Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods
- have been prescribed by a legally qualified medical practitioner
- have been commercially purchased within Australia
- do not have inbuilt cellular connectivity
- can operate independently of mobile devices
- where possible, have Wi-Fi, Bluetooth and other forms of wireless connectivity disabled when operating within SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unauthorised medical devices are not brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unauthorised people are prevented from observing systems, in particular workstation displays and keyboards, within facilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment and media are secured when not in use.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Cyber security awareness training is undertaken annually by all personnel and covers:

- the purpose of the cyber security awareness training
- security appointments and contacts
- authorised use of systems and their resources
- protection of systems and their resources
- reporting of cyber security incidents and suspected compromises of systems and their resources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Tailored privileged user training is undertaken annually by all privileged users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A cyber security awareness training register is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel dealing with banking details and payment requests are advised of what business email compromise is and how to manage and report it.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel dealing with user account details are advised of what social engineering attacks are, how to manage such situations and how to report them.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised of what suspicious contact via online services is and how to report it.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised not to post work-related information on unauthorised online services, and to report cases where such information is posted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised not to post information about their security clearance and briefings on unauthorised online services, and to report cases where such information is posted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised to limit posting information about their work-related duties on unauthorised online services, and to report cases where such information is posted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised to limit posting information about their work-related skills and experience on unauthorised online services, and to report cases where such information is posted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised of security risks associated with posting personal information on online services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised to maintain separate personal user accounts from any work user accounts they use for online services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are encouraged to use any available privacy settings to restrict who can view personal information they post on online services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised not to send or receive files via unauthorised online services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A system usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A general-purpose AI usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A web usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access requirements for systems and their resources are documented in their system security plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel undergo appropriate employment screening and, where necessary, hold an appropriate security clearance before being granted access to systems and their resources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel receive any necessary briefings before being granted access to systems and their resources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel agree to abide by system usage policies before being granted access to systems and their resources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel granted access to systems and their resources are uniquely identifiable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of shared user accounts is strictly controlled, and personnel using such accounts are uniquely identifiable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel who are contractors are identified as such.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where systems process, store or communicate AUSTEO, AGAO or REL data, personnel who are foreign nationals are identified as such, including by their specific nationality.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Requests for unprivileged access to systems and their resources are validated when first requested.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unprivileged access to systems and their resources is limited to only what is required for users and services to undertake their duties.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use of unprivileged access is centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Foreign nationals, including seconded foreign nationals, do not have access to systems that process, store or communicate AUSTEO or REL data unless effective controls are in place to ensure such data is not accessible to them.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Foreign nationals, excluding seconded foreign nationals, do not have access to systems that process, store or communicate AGAO data unless effective controls are in place to ensure such data is not accessible to them.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Requests for privileged access to systems and their resources are validated when first requested.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged access to systems and their resources is limited to only what is required for users and services to undertake their duties.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged user accounts (excluding those explicitly authorised to access online services) are prevented from accessing the internet, email and web services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged user accounts explicitly authorised to access online services are strictly limited to only what is required for users and services to undertake their duties.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Just-in-time administration is used for the administration of systems and their resources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged users are assigned a dedicated privileged user account to be used solely for duties requiring privileged access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unique privileged user accounts are used for administering individual server applications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged access events are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged user account and security group management events are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Foreign nationals, including seconded foreign nationals, do not have privileged access to systems that process, store or communicate AUSTEO or REL data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Foreign nationals, excluding seconded foreign nationals, do not have privileged access to systems that process, store or communicate AGAO data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access to systems and their resources are removed or suspended the same day personnel no longer have a legitimate requirement for access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access to systems and their resources are removed or suspended as soon as practicable when personnel are detected undertaking malicious activities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unprivileged access to systems and their resources are disabled after 45 days of inactivity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged access to systems and their resources are disabled after 45 days of inactivity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged access to systems and their resources are disabled after 12 months unless revalidated.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
A secure record is maintained for the life of systems and their resources that covers the following for each user:

- their user identification
- their signed agreement to abide by system usage policies
- who authorised their access
- when their access was granted
- the level of access they were granted
- when their access, and their level of access, was last reviewed
- when their level of access was changed, and to what extent (if applicable)
- when their access was withdrawn (if applicable).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When personnel are granted temporary access to systems and their resources, effective controls are put in place to restrict their access to only data required for them to undertake their duties.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Temporary access is not granted to systems that process, store or communicate caveated or sensitive compartmented information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A method of emergency access to systems and their resources is documented and tested at least once when initially implemented and each time fundamental information technology infrastructure changes occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Break glass accounts are only used when normal authentication processes cannot be used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Break glass accounts are only used for specific authorised activities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Break glass account credentials are changed by the account custodian after they are accessed by any other party.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Break glass accounts are tested after credentials are changed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use of break glass accounts is centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Systems processing, storing or communicating AUSTEO or AGAO data remain at all times under the control of an Australian national working for or on behalf of the Australian Government.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AUSTEO and AGAO data can only be accessed from systems under the sole control of the Australian Government that are located within facilities authorised by the Australian Government.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cabling infrastructure is installed in accordance with relevant Australian Standards, as directed by the Australian Communications and Media Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Fibre-optic cables are used for cabling infrastructure instead of copper cables.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A cable register is developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
A cable register contains the following for each cable:

- cable identifier
- cable colour
- sensitivity/classification
- source
- destination
- location
- seal numbers (if applicable).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Floor plan diagrams are developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Floor plan diagrams contain the following:

- cable paths (including ingress and egress points between floors)
- cable reticulation system and conduit paths
- floor concentration boxes
- wall outlet boxes
- network cabinets.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cable labelling processes, and supporting cable labelling procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cables are labelled at each end with sufficient source and destination details to enable the physical identification and inspection of the cable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Building management cables are labelled with their purpose in black writing on a yellow background, with a minimum size of 2.5 cm x 1 cm, and attached at five-metre intervals.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cables for foreign systems installed in Australian facilities are labelled at inspection points.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cables for individual systems use a consistent colour.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET cables are coloured red.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET and TOP SECRET cables with non-conformant cable colouring are banded with the appropriate colour and labelled at inspection points.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cables in TOP SECRET areas are fully inspectable for their entire length.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET cables, when bundled together or run in conduit, are run exclusively in their own individual cable bundle or conduit.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cable bundles or conduits sharing a common cable reticulation system have a dividing partition or visible gap between each cable bundle and conduit.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In shared facilities, cables are run in an enclosed cable reticulation system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In shared facilities, conduits or the front covers of ducts, cable trays in floors and ceilings, and associated fittings are clear plastic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In shared facilities, uniquely identifiable SCEC-approved tamper-evident seals are used to seal all removable covers on TOP SECRET cable reticulation systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In shared facilities, a visible smear of conduit glue is used to seal all plastic conduit joints and TOP SECRET conduits connected by threaded lock nuts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Labels for TOP SECRET conduits are a minimum size of 2.5 cm x 1 cm, attached at five-metre intervals and marked as ‘TS RUN’.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cables from cable trays to wall outlet boxes are run in flexible or plastic conduit.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In shared facilities, TOP SECRET cables are not run in party walls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where wall penetrations exit a TOP SECRET area into a lower classified area, TOP SECRET cables are encased in conduit with all gaps between the TOP SECRET conduit and the wall filled with an appropriate sealing compound.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET and TOP SECRET wall outlet boxes contain exclusively SECRET or TOP SECRET cables.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wall outlet boxes denote the systems, cable identifiers and wall outlet box identifier.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wall outlet boxes for individual systems use a consistent colour.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET wall outlet boxes are coloured red.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wall outlet box covers are clear plastic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If TOP SECRET fibre-optic fly leads exceeding five metres in length are used to connect wall outlet boxes to IT equipment, they are run in a protective and easily inspected pathway that is clearly labelled at the IT equipment end with the wall outlet box’s identifier.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets are terminated as close as possible to the cabinet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In TOP SECRET areas, cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets in server rooms or communications rooms are terminated as close as possible to the cabinet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In TOP SECRET areas, cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets not in server rooms or communications rooms are terminated at the boundary of the cabinet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET cables are terminated in an individual TOP SECRET cabinet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET and TOP SECRET cables are terminated on their own individual patch panels.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET patch panels are installed in individual TOP SECRET cabinets.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Where spatial constraints demand non-TOP SECRET patch panels be installed in the same cabinet as a TOP SECRET patch panel:

- a physical barrier in the cabinet is provided to separate patch panels
- only personnel holding a Positive Vetting security clearance have access to the cabinet
- approval from the TOP SECRET system’s authorising officer is obtained prior to installation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A visible gap exists between TOP SECRET cabinets and non-TOP SECRET cabinets.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When penetrating a TOP SECRET audio secure room, the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation is consulted and all directions provided are complied with.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A power distribution board with a feed from an Uninterruptible Power Supply is used to power all TOP SECRET IT equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment meets industry and government standards relating to electromagnetic interference/electromagnetic compatibility.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Emanation security doctrine produced by ASD for the management of emanation security matters is complied with.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners deploying SECRET or TOP SECRET systems within fixed facilities contact ASD for an emanation security risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners deploying SECRET or TOP SECRET systems in mobile platforms, or as a deployable capability, contact ASD for an emanation security risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When an emanation security risk assessment is required, it is sought as early as possible in a system’s life cycle.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Recommended actions contained within emanation security mitigation advice issued for systems are implemented by system owners.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A telephone system usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised of the permitted sensitivity or classification of information that can be discussed over internal and external telephone systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised of security risks posed by non-secure telephone systems in areas where sensitive or classified conversations can occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using cryptographic equipment to permit different levels of conversation for different kinds of connections, telephone systems give a visual indication of what kind of connection has been made.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Telephone systems used for sensitive or classified conversations encrypt all traffic that passes over external systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cordless telephone handsets and headsets are not used for sensitive or classified conversations unless all communications are encrypted using ASD-approved cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Speakerphones are not used on telephone systems in TOP SECRET areas unless the telephone system is located in an audio secure room, the room is audio secure during conversations and only personnel involved in conversations are present in the room.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Off-hook audio protection features are used on telephone systems in areas where background conversations may exceed the sensitivity or classification that the telephone system is authorised for communicating.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In SECRET and TOP SECRET areas, push-to-talk handsets or push-to-talk headsets are used to meet any off-hook audio protection requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Video conferencing and IP telephony infrastructure is hardened.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When video conferencing or IP telephony traffic passes through a gateway containing a firewall or proxy, a video-aware or voice-aware firewall or proxy is used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Video conferencing and IP telephony calls are established using a secure session initiation protocol.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Video conferencing and IP telephony calls are conducted using a secure real-time transport protocol.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An encrypted and non-replayable two-way authentication scheme is used for call authentication and authorisation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authentication and authorisation is used for all actions on a video conferencing network, including call setup and changing settings.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authentication and authorisation is used for all actions on an IP telephony network, including registering a new IP phone, changing phone users, changing settings and accessing voicemail.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
IP telephony is configured such that:

- IP phones authenticate themselves to the call controller upon registration
- auto-registration is disabled and only authorised devices are allowed to access the network
- unauthorised devices are blocked by default
- all unused and prohibited functionality is disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Individual logins are implemented for IP phones used for SECRET or TOP SECRET conversations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Video conferencing and IP telephony traffic is physically or logically separated from other data traffic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Workstations are not connected to video conferencing units or IP phones unless the workstation or the device uses Virtual Local Area Networks or similar mechanisms to maintain separation between video conferencing, IP telephony and other data traffic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IP phones used in public areas do not have the ability to access data networks, voicemail and directory services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A denial of service response plan for video conferencing and IP telephony services is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
A denial of service response plan for video conferencing and IP telephony services contains the following:

- how to identify signs of a denial-of-service attack
- how to identify the source of a denial-of-service attack
- how capabilities can be maintained during a denial-of-service attack
- what actions can be taken to respond to a denial-of-service attack.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An MFD usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: MFDs are not connected to digital telephone systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users authenticate to MFDs before they can print, scan or copy documents.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authentication measures for MFDs are the same strength as those used for workstations on networks they are connected to.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: MFDs are not used to scan or copy documents above the sensitivity or classification of networks they are connected to.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use of MFDs for printing, scanning and copying purposes, including the capture of shadow copies of documents, are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: MFDs are placed in areas where their use can be observed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Fax machines, and online fax services, are not used for sending or receiving fax messages.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Legal advice is sought prior to allowing privately owned mobile devices and desktop computers to access systems or data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privately owned mobile devices and desktop computers do not access SECRET and TOP SECRET systems or data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel using organisation-owned mobile devices or desktop computers to access classified systems or data have enforced separation of classified data and personal data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices and desktop computers access the internet via an organisation’s internet gateway rather than via a direct connection to the internet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When accessing an organisation’s network via a VPN connection, split tunnelling is disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A mobile device management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile Device Management solutions that have completed a Common Criteria evaluation against the Protection Profile for Mobile Device Management, version 4.0 or later, are used to enforce mobile device management policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices that access SECRET or TOP SECRET systems or data use mobile platforms that have been issued an Approval for Use by ASD and are operated in accordance with the latest version of their associated Australian Communications Security Instruction.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices encrypt their internal storage and any removable media using ASD-approved cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET and TOP SECRET mobile devices do not use removable media unless approved beforehand by ASD.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices encrypt all sensitive or classified data communicated over public network infrastructure using ASD-approved cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile applications encrypt all sensitive or classified data communicated over public network infrastructure using ASD-approved cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are configured to operate in a supervised (or equivalent) mode.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are configured to enforce separation between organisational and personal mobile applications and data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are configured with always on VPN functionality.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are configured with remote locate and wipe functionality.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are configured with secure password-based lock screens.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are configured to prevent data transfers over Universal Serial Bus connections.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices prevent personnel from installing non-approved applications once provisioned.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices prevent personnel from disabling or modifying security functionality once provisioned.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security updates are applied to mobile devices as soon as they become available.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A mobile device usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised of the sensitivity or classification permitted for voice and data communications when using mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Personnel are advised to take the following precautions when using mobile devices:

- never leave mobile devices or removable media unattended, including by placing them in checked-in luggage or leaving them in hotel safes
- never store credentials with mobile devices that they grant access to, such as in laptop computer bags
- never lend mobile devices or removable media to untrusted people, even if briefly
- never allow untrusted people to connect their mobile devices or removable media to your mobile devices, including for charging
- never connect mobile devices to designated charging stations or wall outlet charging ports
- never use gifted or unauthorised peripherals, chargers or removable media with mobile devices
- never use removable media for data transfers or backups that have not been checked for malicious code beforehand
- avoid reuse of removable media once used with other parties’ systems or mobile devices
- avoid connecting mobile devices to open or untrusted Wi-Fi networks
- consider disabling any communications capabilities of mobile devices when not in use, such as Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Near Field Communication and ultra-wideband
- consider periodically rebooting mobile devices
- consider using a VPN connection to encrypt all cellular and wireless communications
- consider using encrypted email or messaging apps for all communications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Paging, Multimedia Message Service, Short Message Service and messaging apps are not used to communicate sensitive or classified data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Bluetooth functionality is not enabled on SECRET and TOP SECRET mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are not connected to the infotainment systems of connected vehicles.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Sensitive or classified data is not viewed on mobile devices within or near connected vehicles.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Sensitive or classified phone calls and conversations are not conducted within or near connected vehicles.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Sensitive or classified data is not viewed on mobile devices in public locations unless care is taken to reduce the chance of the screen of a mobile device being observed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privacy filters are applied to the screens of SECRET and TOP SECRET mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Sensitive or classified phone calls and conversations are not conducted in public locations unless care is taken to reduce the chance of conversations being overheard.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are kept under continual direct supervision when being actively used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices are carried or stored in a secured state when not being actively used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If unable to carry or store mobile devices in a secured state, they are physically transferred in a security briefcase or an approved multi-use satchel, pouch or transit bag.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile device emergency sanitisation processes, and supporting mobile device emergency sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If a cryptographic zeroise or sanitise function is provided for cryptographic keys on a SECRET or TOP SECRET mobile device, the function is used as part of mobile device emergency sanitisation processes and procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel are advised of privacy and security risks when travelling overseas with mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
If travelling overseas with mobile devices to high or extreme risk countries, personnel are:

- issued with newly provisioned user accounts, mobile devices and removable media from a pool of dedicated travel devices which are used solely for work-related activities
- advised on how to apply and inspect tamper seals to key areas of mobile devices
- advised to avoid taking any personal mobile devices, especially if rooted or jailbroken.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Before travelling overseas with mobile devices, personnel take the following actions:

- record all details of the mobile devices being taken, such as product types, serial numbers and International Mobile Equipment Identity numbers
- update all operating systems and applications
- remove all non-essential data, applications and user accounts
- backup all remaining data, applications and settings.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Personnel report the potential compromise of mobile devices, removable media or credentials to their organisation as soon as possible, especially if they:

- provide credentials to foreign government officials
- decrypt mobile devices for foreign government officials
- have mobile devices taken out of sight by foreign government officials
- have mobile devices or removable media stolen, including if later returned
- lose mobile devices or removable media, including if later found
- observe unusual behaviour of mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Upon returning from travelling overseas with mobile devices, personnel take the following actions:

- sanitise and reset mobile devices, including all removable media
- decommission any credentials that left their possession during their travel
- report if significant doubt exists as to the integrity of any mobile devices or removable media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
If returning from travelling overseas with mobile devices to high or extreme risk countries, personnel take the following additional actions:

- reset credentials used with mobile devices, including those used for remote access to their organisation’s systems
- monitor user accounts for any indicators of compromise, such as failed logon attempts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If procuring an evaluated product, a product that has completed a PP-based evaluation, including against all applicable PP modules (as well as a software bill of materials assessment if applicable), is selected in preference to one that has completed an EAL-based evaluation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Evaluated products are delivered in a manner consistent with any delivery procedures defined in associated evaluation documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When procuring high assurance information technology (IT) equipment, ASD is contacted for any equipment-specific delivery procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Evaluated products are installed, configured, administered and operated in an evaluated configuration and in accordance with vendor guidance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: High assurance IT equipment is installed, configured, administered and operated in an evaluated configuration and in accordance with ASD guidance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An IT equipment management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Approved configurations for IT equipment are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment is hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A networked IT equipment register is developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A non-networked IT equipment register is developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment, except for high assurance IT equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ASD’s approval is sought before applying labels to external surfaces of high assurance IT equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment is classified based on the highest sensitivity or classification of data that it is approved for processing, storing or communicating.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment is handled in a manner suitable for its sensitivity or classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ASD’s approval is sought before undertaking any maintenance or repairs to high assurance IT equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Maintenance or repairs of IT equipment are carried out on site by an appropriately cleared technician.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If an appropriately cleared technician is not used to undertake maintenance or repairs to IT equipment, the IT equipment and associated media are sanitised before maintenance or repairs.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
If an appropriately cleared technician is not used to undertake maintenance or repairs to IT equipment, the technician is escorted by someone who:

- has the authority to direct the technician
- is appropriately cleared and briefed
- is sufficiently familiar with the IT equipment to understand the work being undertaken
- takes all responsible measures to ensure the integrity of the IT equipment
- takes due care to ensure that data is not disclosed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment maintained or repaired off site is handled at facilities approved for handling the sensitivity or classification of the IT equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following maintenance or repairs to IT equipment, it is inspected to confirm that it retains its approved configuration and that no unauthorised modifications have been made.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment sanitisation processes, and supporting IT equipment sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment destruction processes, and supporting IT equipment destruction procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment containing media is sanitised by removing the media from the IT equipment or by sanitising the media in situ.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment that cannot be sanitised is destroyed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment, including associated media, that is located overseas and has processed, stored or communicated AUSTEO or AGAO data, is sanitised in situ.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment, including associated media, that is located overseas and has processed, stored or communicated AUSTEO or AGAO data that cannot be sanitised in situ, is returned to Australia for destruction.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: High assurance IT equipment is destroyed prior to its disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: At least three pages of random text with no blank areas are printed on each colour printer cartridge or MFD print drum.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: MFD print drums and image transfer rollers are inspected and destroyed if there is remnant toner that cannot be removed or a print is visible on the image transfer roller.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Printer and MFD platens are inspected and destroyed if any text or images are retained on the platen.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Printers and MFDs are checked to ensure no pages are trapped in the paper path due to a paper jam.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When unable to sanitise printer cartridges or MFD print drums, they are destroyed as per electrostatic memory devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Printer ribbons in printers and MFDs are removed and destroyed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Televisions and computer monitors with minor burn-in or image persistence are sanitised by displaying a solid white image on the screen for an extended period.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Televisions and computer monitors that cannot be sanitised are destroyed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Memory in network devices is sanitised using the following processes, in order of preference:

- following device-specific guidance provided in evaluation documentation
- following vendor sanitisation guidance
- loading a dummy configuration file, performing a factory reset and then reinstalling firmware.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment disposal processes, and supporting IT equipment disposal procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Labels and markings indicating the owner, sensitivity, classification or any other marking that can associate IT equipment with its prior use are removed prior to its disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When disposing of IT equipment that has been designed or modified to meet emanation security standards, ASD is contacted for requirements relating to its disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following sanitisation, destruction or declassification, a formal administrative decision is made to release IT equipment, or its waste, into the public domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A media management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A removable media usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A removable media register is developed, implemented, maintained and regularly verified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media, except for internally mounted fixed media within information technology equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media is classified to the highest sensitivity or classification of data it stores, unless the media has been classified to a higher sensitivity or classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media is only used with systems that are authorised to process, store or communicate its sensitivity or classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any media connected to a system with a higher sensitivity or classification than the media is reclassified to the higher sensitivity or classification, unless the media is read-only or the system has a mechanism through which read-only access can be ensured.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Before reclassifying media to a lower sensitivity or classification, the media is sanitised or destroyed, and a formal administrative decision is made to reclassify it.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All data stored on media is encrypted using ASD-approved cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Full disk encryption, or partial encryption where access controls only allow writing to encrypted partitions or volumes, is implemented when encrypting media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Pre-boot authentication using passwords, or managed network-based key release, is implemented for media containing encrypted system volumes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media is handled in a manner suitable for its sensitivity or classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media is sanitised before it is used for the first time.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media is sanitised before it is reused in a different security domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When transferring data manually between two systems belonging to different security domains, write-once media is used unless the destination system has a mechanism through which read-only access can be ensured.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When transferring data manually between two systems belonging to different security domains, rewritable media is sanitised after each data transfer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media sanitisation processes, and supporting media sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Volatile media is sanitised by removing its power for at least 10 minutes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET and TOP SECRET volatile media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following sanitisation, TOP SECRET volatile media retains its classification if it stored static data for an extended period, or had data repeatedly stored on or written to the same memory location for an extended period.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-volatile magnetic media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once (or three times if pre-2001 or under 15 GB) in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The host-protected area and device configuration overlay table are reset prior to the sanitisation of non-volatile magnetic hard drives.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The ATA secure erase command is used, in addition to block overwriting software, to ensure the growth defects table of non-volatile magnetic hard drives is overwritten.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile magnetic media retains its classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-volatile EPROM media is sanitised by applying three times the manufacturer’s specified ultraviolet erasure time and then overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-volatile EEPROM media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile EPROM and EEPROM media retains its classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-volatile flash memory media is sanitised by overwriting it at least twice in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile flash memory media retains its classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media that cannot be successfully sanitised is destroyed prior to its disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media destruction processes, and supporting media destruction procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
The following media types are destroyed prior to their disposal:

- microfiche and microfilm
- optical discs
- programmable read-only memory
- read-only memory
- other types of media that cannot be sanitised.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security Construction and Equipment Committee-approved equipment or ASIO-approved equipment is used when destroying media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If using degaussers to destroy media, degaussers evaluated by the United States’ National Security Agency are used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Equipment that is capable of reducing microform to a fine powder, with resultant particles not showing more than five consecutive characters per particle upon microscopic inspection, is used to destroy microfiche and microfilm.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Electrostatic memory devices are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator or grinder/sander.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Magnetic floppy disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, degausser or by cutting.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Magnetic hard disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or degausser.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Magnetic tapes are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, degausser or by cutting.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Optical disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or by cutting.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Semiconductor memory is destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill or disintegrator.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media destroyed using a hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or by cutting results in media waste particles no larger than 9 mm.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The resulting media waste particles from the destruction of TOP SECRET media is stored and handled as OFFICIAL if less than or equal to 3 mm, or SECRET if greater than 3 mm and less than or equal to 9 mm.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Magnetic media is destroyed using a degausser with a suitable magnetic field strength and magnetic orientation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Product-specific directions provided by degausser manufacturers are followed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following the use of a degausser, magnetic media is physically damaged by deforming any internal platters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The destruction of media is performed under the supervision of at least one cleared person.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel supervising the destruction of media supervise its handling to the point of destruction and ensure that the destruction is completed successfully.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The destruction of media storing accountable material is performed under the supervision of at least two cleared personnel.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel supervising the destruction of media storing accountable material supervise its handling to the point of destruction, ensure that the destruction is completed successfully and sign a destruction certificate afterwards.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The destruction of media storing accountable material is not outsourced.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media disposal processes, and supporting media disposal procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Labels and markings indicating the owner, sensitivity, classification or any other marking that can associate media with its prior use are removed prior to its disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following sanitisation, destruction or declassification, a formal administrative decision is made to release media, or its waste, into the public domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Vendors that have demonstrated a commitment to Secure by Design and Secure by Default principles and practices, including secure programming practices and either memory-safe programming languages or less preferably memory-safe programming practices, are used for operating systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The latest release, or the previous release, of operating systems are used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where supported, 64-bit versions of operating systems are used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SOEs are used for workstations and servers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SOEs provided by third parties are scanned for malicious code and configurations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SOEs are reviewed and updated at least annually.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Approved configurations for operating systems are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Operating systems are hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft’s attack surface reduction rules are implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Default user accounts or credentials for operating systems, including for any pre-configured user accounts, are changed, disabled or removed during initial setup.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unneeded user accounts, components, services and functionality of operating systems are disabled or removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Automatic execution features for removable media are disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Internet Explorer 11 is disabled or removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: .NET Framework 3.5 (includes .NET 2.0 and 3.0) is disabled or removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Operating system exploit protection functionality is enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Early Launch Antimalware, Secure Boot, Trusted Boot and Measured Boot functionality is enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unprivileged users are prevented from bypassing, disabling or modifying security functionality of operating systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Unprivileged users are prevented from running script execution engines, including:

- Windows Script Host (cscript.exe and wscript.exe)
- PowerShell (powershell.exe, powershell_ise.exe and pwsh.exe)
- Command Prompt (cmd.exe)
- Windows Management Instrumentation (wmic.exe)
- Microsoft Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) Application Host (mshta.exe).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unprivileged users do not have the ability to install unapproved applications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unprivileged users do not have the ability to uninstall or disable approved applications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control is implemented on workstations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control is implemented on internet-facing servers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control is implemented on non-internet-facing servers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control is applied to user profiles and temporary folders used by operating systems, web browsers and email clients.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control is applied to all locations other than user profiles and temporary folders used by operating systems, web browsers and email clients.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control restricts the execution of executables, libraries, scripts, installers, compiled HTML, HTML applications and control panel applets to an organisation-approved set.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control restricts the execution of drivers to an organisation-approved set.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control is implemented using cryptographic hash rules, publisher certificate rules or path rules.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When implementing application control using publisher certificate rules, publisher names and product names are used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When implementing application control using path rules, only approved users can modify approved files and write to approved folders.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When implementing application control using path rules, only approved users can change file system permissions for approved files and folders.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft’s recommended application blocklist is implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft’s vulnerable driver blocklist is implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Application control rulesets are validated at least annually.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All users, except for local administrator accounts and break glass accounts, cannot disable, bypass or be exempted from application control.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Allowed and blocked application control events are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Command line process creation events are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Windows PowerShell 2.0 is disabled or removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PowerShell is configured to use Constrained Language Mode.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PowerShell module logging, script block logging and transcription events are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PowerShell script block logs are protected by Protected Event Logging functionality.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A HIPS or EDR solution is implemented on workstations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A HIPS or EDR solution is implemented on critical servers and high-value servers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A software firewall is implemented on workstations and servers to restrict inbound and outbound network connections to an organisation-approved set of applications and services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
An antivirus application is implemented on workstations and servers with:

- signature-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level
- heuristic-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level
- reputation rating functionality enabled
- ransomware protection functionality enabled
- detection signatures configured to update at least daily
- regular scanning configured for all fixed disks and removable media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If there is no business requirement for reading from removable media and devices, such functionality is disabled via the use of a device access control application or by disabling external communication interfaces.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If there is no business requirement for writing to removable media and devices, such functionality is disabled via the use of a device access control application or by disabling external communication interfaces.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: External communication interfaces that allow DMA are disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security-relevant events for Apple macOS operating systems are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security-relevant events for Linux operating systems are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security-relevant events for Microsoft Windows operating systems are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Vendors that have demonstrated a commitment to Secure by Design and Secure by Default principles and practices, including secure programming practices and either memory-safe programming languages or less preferably memory-safe programming practices, are used for user applications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The latest release of email clients, office productivity suites, PDF applications, security products and web browsers, including their extensions, are used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Approved configurations for user applications are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User applications are hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Default user accounts or credentials for user applications, including for any pre-configured user accounts, are changed, disabled or removed during initial setup.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unneeded user accounts, components, services and functionality of user applications are disabled or removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Extensions for user applications are restricted to an organisation-approved set.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All temporary installation files created during user application installation processes are removed after user applications have been installed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI applications that process classified data have their ability to directly access external public data sources disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI applications are configured to flag organisationally defined risky actions for human approval prior to their execution.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Baselines of expected behaviour and performance for AI applications are established and monitored for unexpected deviations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Email client security settings cannot be changed by users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Office productivity suites are hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office is blocked from creating child processes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office is blocked from creating executable content.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office is blocked from injecting code into other processes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office is configured to prevent activation of Object Linking and Embedding packages.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Office productivity suite security settings cannot be changed by users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macros are disabled for users that do not have a demonstrated business requirement.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macros in files originating from the internet are blocked.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macro antivirus scanning is enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macros are blocked from making Win32 API calls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Only Microsoft Office macros running from within a sandboxed environment, a Trusted Location or that are digitally signed by a trusted publisher are allowed to execute.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macros are checked to ensure they are free of malicious code before being digitally signed or placed within Trusted Locations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Only privileged users responsible for checking that Microsoft Office macros are free of malicious code can write to and modify content within Trusted Locations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macros digitally signed by an untrusted publisher cannot be enabled via the Message Bar or Backstage View.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macros digitally signed by signatures other than V3 signatures cannot be enabled via the Message Bar or Backstage View.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office’s list of trusted publishers is validated at least annually.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft Office macro security settings cannot be changed by users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PDF applications are blocked from creating child processes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PDF applications are hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PDF application security settings cannot be changed by users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security product security settings cannot be changed by users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Web browsers do not process Java from the internet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Web browsers do not process web advertisements from the internet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Web browsers are hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Web browser security settings cannot be changed by users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Vendors that have demonstrated a commitment to Secure by Design and Secure by Default principles and practices, including secure programming practices and either memory-safe programming languages or less preferably memory-safe programming practices, are used for server applications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The latest release of internet-facing server applications is used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Approved configurations for server applications are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Server applications are hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Default user accounts or credentials for server applications, including for any pre-configured user accounts, are changed, disabled or removed during initial setup.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unneeded user accounts, components, services and functionality of server applications are disabled or removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Extensions for server applications are restricted to an organisation-approved set.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All temporary installation files created during server application installation processes are removed after server applications have been installed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Server applications are configured to run as a separate user account with the minimum privileges needed to perform their functions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The user accounts under which server applications run have limited access to their underlying server’s file system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft AD DS domain controllers, Microsoft AD CS CA servers, Microsoft AD FS servers and Microsoft Entra Connect servers are only used for their designed role and no other applications or services are installed, unless they are security related.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access to Microsoft AD DS domain controllers, Microsoft AD CS CA servers, Microsoft AD FS servers and Microsoft Entra Connect servers is limited to privileged users that require access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Backups of Microsoft AD DS domain controllers, Microsoft AD CS CA servers, Microsoft AD FS servers and Microsoft Entra Connect servers are encrypted using ASD-approved cryptography, stored securely and only accessible to backup administrator accounts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security-relevant events for Microsoft AD DS domain controllers, Microsoft AD CS CA servers, Microsoft AD FS servers and Microsoft Entra Connect servers are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft AD DS domain controllers are administered using dedicated domain administrator user accounts that are not used to administer other systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Lightweight Directory Access Protocol signing is enabled on Microsoft AD DS domain controllers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Print Spooler service is disabled on Microsoft AD DS domain controllers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Passwords are not stored in Group Policy Preferences.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Passwords are prevented from being stored in Group Policy Preferences.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SID Filtering is enabled for domain and forest trusts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Only service accounts and computer accounts are configured with Service Principal Names (SPNs).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The number of service accounts configured with an SPN is minimised.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Service accounts configured with an SPN do not have DCSync permissions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Service accounts configured with an SPN use the Advanced Encryption Standard for encryption.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Duplicate SPNs do not exist within the domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User accounts are provisioned with the minimum privileges required.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User accounts with DCSync permissions are reviewed at least annually, and those without an ongoing requirement for the permissions have them removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged user accounts are configured as sensitive and cannot be delegated.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Computer accounts are not configured for unconstrained delegation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User accounts require Kerberos pre-authentication.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The UserPassword attribute for user accounts is not used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The sIDHistory attribute for user accounts is not used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User accounts are checked at least weekly for the presence of the sIDHistory attribute.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Account properties accessible by unprivileged users are not used to store passwords.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User account passwords do not use reversible encryption.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unprivileged user accounts cannot add machines to the domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Dedicated privileged service accounts are used to add machines to the domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User accounts with unconstrained delegation are reviewed at least annually, and those without an SPN or demonstrated business requirement are removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Computer accounts that are not Microsoft AD DS domain controllers are not trusted for delegation to services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Domain Computers security group does not have write or modify permissions to any Microsoft Active Directory objects.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged user accounts are members of the Protected Users security group.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The number of user accounts that are members of the Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins or other highly privileged security groups is minimised.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Service accounts are not members of the Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins or other highly privileged security groups.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Computer accounts are not members of the Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins or other highly privileged security groups.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Domain Computers security group is not a member of any privileged or highly privileged security groups.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When a user account is disabled, it is removed from all security group memberships.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Pre-Windows 2000 Compatible Access security group does not contain user accounts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Strong mapping between certificates and users is enforced.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 flag is removed from Microsoft AD CS CA configurations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT flag is removed from certificate templates.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unprivileged user accounts do not have write access to certificate templates.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Extended Key Usages that enable user authentication are removed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: CA Certificate Manager approval is required for certificate templates that allow a Subject Alternative Name to be supplied.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microsoft AD FS servers are administered using a dedicated service account that is not used to administer other systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Soft matching between Microsoft AD DS and Microsoft Entra ID is disabled following initial synchronisation activities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Hard match takeover is disabled for Microsoft Entra Connect servers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privileged user accounts are not synchronised between Microsoft AD DS and Microsoft Entra ID.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security-relevant events for server applications on internet-facing servers are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security-relevant events for server applications on non-internet-facing servers are centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users are authenticated before they are granted access to a system and its resources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authentication methods susceptible to replay attacks are disabled.

parts.name: statement
