parts.prose: Web content filtering is applied to outbound web traffic where appropriate.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TLS traffic communicated through gateways is decrypted and inspected.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An organisation-approved list of domain names, or list of website categories, is implemented for all Hypertext Transfer Protocol and Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure traffic communicated through gateways.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Malicious domain names, dynamic domain names and domain names that can be registered anonymously for free are blocked by web content filters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Attempts to access websites through their IP addresses instead of their domain names are blocked by web content filters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content filtering checks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files identified by content filtering checks as malicious, or that cannot be inspected, are blocked.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files identified by content filtering checks as suspicious are quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Content filters used by CDSs undergo rigorous security testing to ensure they perform as expected and cannot be bypassed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Encrypted files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are decrypted to undergo content filtering checks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Archive files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are unpacked to undergo content filtering checks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Archive files are unpacked in a controlled manner to ensure content filter performance or availability is not adversely affected.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo antivirus scanning using multiple different scanning engines.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Executable files imported via gateways or CDSs are automatically executed in a sandbox to detect any suspicious behaviour.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are filtered for allowed file types.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content validation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content checking.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content conversion.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content sanitisation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs that have a digital signature or cryptographic checksum are validated.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Evaluated peripheral switches are used when sharing peripherals between systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Evaluated peripheral switches used for sharing peripherals between SECRET and TOP SECRET systems, or between SECRET or TOP SECRET systems belonging to different security domains, preferably complete a high assurance evaluation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Evaluated peripheral switches used for sharing peripherals between SECRET or TOP SECRET systems and any non-SECRET or TOP SECRET systems complete a high assurance evaluation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data transfer processes, and supporting data transfer procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Processes, and supporting procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained to prevent AUSTEO, AGAO and REL data in textual and non-textual formats from being exported to unsuitable foreign systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users transferring data to and from systems are held accountable for data transfers they perform.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When manually importing data to systems, the data is scanned for malicious and active content.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When manually importing data to systems, all data that fails security checks is quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data exported from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems is reviewed and authorised by a trustworthy source beforehand.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data authorised for export from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems is digitally signed by a trustworthy source.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Trustworthy sources for SECRET and TOP SECRET systems are limited to people and services that have been verified and authorised as such by the chief information security officer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When manually exporting data from systems, the data is checked for unsuitable protective markings.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When manually exporting data from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems, digital signatures are validated and keyword checks are performed within all textual data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When manually exporting data from systems, all data that fails security checks is quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data transfer logs are used to record all data imports and exports from systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data transfer logs for systems are partially verified at least monthly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data transfer logs for SECRET and TOP SECRET systems are fully verified at least monthly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners obtain an authorisation to operate for each non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET system from its authorising officer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET gateways undergo an IRAP assessment, using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the IRAP assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET cables, when bundled together or run in conduit, are run exclusively in their own individual cable bundle or conduit.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Passwords used for single-factor authentication on non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED systems are a minimum of 15 characters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cryptographic equipment, applications or libraries that have completed a Common Criteria evaluation against an ASD-endorsed Protection Profile are used when encrypting media that contains OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cryptographic equipment, applications or libraries that have completed a Common Criteria evaluation against an ASD-endorsed Protection Profile are used to protect OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED data when communicated over insufficiently secure networks, outside of appropriately secure areas or via public network infrastructure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using DH for agreeing on encryption session keys, a modulus of at least 2048 bits is used, preferably 3072 bits.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using ECDH for agreeing on encryption session keys, a base point order and key size of at least 224 bits is used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using ECDSA for digital signatures, a base point order and key size of at least 224 bits is used, preferably the P-384 curve.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using RSA for digital signatures, and transporting encryption session keys (and similar keys), a modulus of at least 2048 bits is used, preferably 3072 bits.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microphones (including headsets and USB handsets) and webcams are not used with non-SECRET workstations in SECRET areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When outsourcing the destruction of media storing non-accountable material, a National Association for Information Destruction AAA certified destruction service with endorsements, as specified in ASIO’s Protective Security Circular-167, is used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED cables are coloured neither salmon pink nor red.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET cables are terminated in an individual cabinet; or for small systems, a cabinet with a division plate between any SECRET cables and non-SECRET cables.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED wall outlet boxes are coloured neither salmon pink nor red.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cables in non-TOP SECRET areas are inspectable every five metres or less.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED mobile devices are configured to remain undiscoverable to other Bluetooth devices except during Bluetooth pairing.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Bluetooth pairing for non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED mobile devices is performed in a manner such that connections are only made between intended Bluetooth devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Bluetooth pairings for non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED mobile devices are removed when there is no longer a requirement for their use.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Bluetooth pairing for non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED mobile devices is performed using Secure Connections, preferably with Numeric Comparison if supported.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel using privately owned mobile devices or desktop computers to access OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED systems or data have enforced separation of classified data and personal data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Microphones (including headsets and USB handsets) and webcams are not used with non-TOP SECRET workstations in TOP SECRET areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Passwords used for single-factor authentication on SECRET systems are a minimum of 17 characters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Passwords used for multi-factor authentication on non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED systems are a minimum of 6 characters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Passwords used for multi-factor authentication on SECRET systems are a minimum of 8 characters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Outsourced cloud service providers and their non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET cloud services undergo an IRAP assessment, using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the IRAP assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners, in consultation with each system’s authorising officer, ensure controls for each non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET system and its operating environment undergo a security assessment by their organisation’s own assessors or Infosec Registered Assessor Program (IRAP) assessors to determine if they have been implemented correctly and are operating as intended.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET cables are coloured salmon pink.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SECRET wall outlet boxes are coloured salmon pink.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The resulting media waste particles from the destruction of SECRET media is stored and handled as OFFICIAL if less than or equal to 3 mm, PROTECTED if greater than 3 mm and less than or equal to 6 mm, or SECRET if greater than 6 mm and less than or equal to 9 mm.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using ECDH for agreeing on encryption session keys, NIST P-256, P-384 or P-521 curves are used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using ECDSA for digital signatures, NIST P-256, P-384 or P-521 curves are used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using SHA-2 for hashing, an output size of at least 224 bits is used, preferably SHA-384 or SHA-512.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using SHA-2 for hashing, an output size of at least 256 bits is used, preferably SHA-384 or SHA-512.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using AES for encryption, AES-128, AES-192 or AES-256 is used, preferably AES-256.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Managed service providers and their non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET managed services undergo an Infosec Registered Assessor Program (IRAP) assessment, using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the IRAP assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel using privately owned mobile devices or desktop computers to access OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED systems or data are prevented from storing classified data on their privately owned mobile devices and desktop computers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices that access OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED systems or data use mobile platforms that have completed a Common Criteria evaluation against the Protection Profile for Mobile Device Fundamentals, version 3.3 or later, and are operated in accordance with the latest version of their associated ASD security configuration guide.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-classified systems are secured in suitably secure facilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in suitably secure server rooms or communications rooms.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in suitably secure security containers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel using privately owned mobile devices or desktop computers to access OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED systems or data are disallowed from granting access to unapproved artificial intelligence agents.
